perturbation.original_text: a. "Affiliate" means an entity, directly or indirectly, controlled by, controlling of, or under common control with a party, either now or in the future, and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: a. "Affiliate" means an entity directly controlled by a party. The definition applies solely to subsidiaries fully owned by either WPT or Zynga.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly narrows the definition of 'Affiliate.' The original definition included entities under 'common control,' allowing for broader interpretation, potentially including sister companies or entities with shared ownership. The modified definition restricts it to 'directly controlled' entities, specifically 'subsidiaries fully owned.' This creates an in-text contradiction because other clauses regarding indemnification and liability limitations involving affiliates could be interpreted differently now. For instance, if a sister company (under common control but not a fully owned subsidiary) takes action that leads to liability, the indemnification clauses may no longer apply, conflicting with the original intent of the broader definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a. WPT shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Zynga and its Affiliates, and the respective directors, officers and employees of the foregoing (the "Zynga Indemnified Parties") from and against any and all third party claims, actions, suits, costs, liabilities, judgments, obligations, losses, penalties, expenses or damages (including, without limitation, reasonable legal fees and expenses) of whatsoever kind and nature imposed on, incurred by or asserted against any of the Zynga Indemnified Parties arising out of: (i) any breach or alleged breach by WPT of any representation, warranty or covenant made, by WPT pursuant to this Agreement; or (ii) WPT's non-compliance with any applicable federal, state or local laws or with any applicable regulations in connection with its performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Affiliate' significantly narrows its scope from entities 'under common control' to only 'directly controlled' subsidiaries. This directly contradicts the indemnification clause, which covers Zynga and its 'Affiliates.' Since a sister company under common control would no longer be considered an Affiliate under the modified definition, the indemnification clause's protection would not extend to them, creating a clear contradiction and potentially limiting Zynga's protection. This fulfills the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' because essential language was altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations in another section.

perturbation.original_text: e. "Annual Minimum Guarantee" means the guaranteed minimum amount due to WPT by Zynga in consideration of the rights granted herein, which amount may be recoupable from Royalties as set forth below and in the Basic Provisions.
perturbation.changed_text: e. "Annual Minimum Guarantee" means the amount due to WPT by Zynga in consideration of the rights granted herein.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'guaranteed minimum amount' and 'which amount may be recoupable from Royalties as set forth below and in the Basic Provisions,' this modification introduces ambiguity. The original definition clearly establishes the Annual Minimum Guarantee as a guaranteed minimum that Zynga is obligated to pay, regardless of royalty earnings, and clarifies how it relates to royalties. The modified version only mentions 'the amount due' which no longer explicitly mentions that this is a guaranteed minimum, or if it can be recoupable from royalties as it was previously established, creating uncertainty about the payment obligations. This contradicts the section that mentions the Annual Minimum Guarantee functions as an advance payment, since it's now not guaranteed or recoupable as it was mentioned originally.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a. Annual Minimum Guarantee. Zynga will pay to WPT the Annual Minimum Guarantee as set forth in the Basic Provisions. The Annual Minimum Guarantee shall be recoupable from such Royalties as are, or have become, paid to WPT. For clarification, the Annual Minimum Guarantee will operate as an advance payment, such that when accrued Royalties exceed the Annual Minimum Guarantee payments already paid, then the excess Royalties will be paid by Zynga to WPT.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the phrases 'guaranteed minimum amount' and 'which amount may be recoupable from Royalties as set forth below and in the Basic Provisions,' which introduces ambiguity. The original text explicitly states that the Annual Minimum Guarantee is a guaranteed minimum, recoupable from royalties. The changed version removes this guarantee and the recoupability aspect, contradicting the contradicted text, which states, 'The Annual Minimum Guarantee shall be recoupable from such Royalties as are, or have become, paid to WPT,' and 'the Annual Minimum Guarantee will operate as an advance payment.' Therefore, the modified text creates a contradiction by removing the guaranteed nature and recoupability, which were established in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: 5. INDEMNIFICATION a. WPT shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Zynga and its Affiliates, and the respective directors, officers and employees of the foregoing (the "Zynga Indemnified Parties") from and against any and all third party claims, actions, suits, costs, liabilities, judgments, obligations, losses, penalties, expenses or damages (including, without limitation, reasonable legal fees and expenses) of whatsoever kind and nature imposed on, incurred by or asserted against any of the Zynga Indemnified Parties arising out of: (i) any breach or alleged breach by WPT of any representation, warranty or covenant made, by WPT pursuant to this Agreement; or (ii) WPT's non-compliance with any applicable federal, state or local laws or with any applicable regulations in connection with its performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. INDEMNIFICATION a. WPT shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Zynga and its Affiliates, and the respective directors, officers and employees of the foregoing (the "Zynga Indemnified Parties") from and against any and all third party claims, actions, suits, costs, liabilities, judgments, obligations, losses, penalties, expenses or damages (including, without limitation, reasonable legal fees and expenses) of whatsoever kind and nature imposed on, incurred by or asserted against any of the Zynga Indemnified Parties arising out of: (i) any breach or alleged breach by WPT of any representation, warranty or covenant made, by WPT pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting clause (ii) 'WPT's non-compliance with any applicable federal, state or local laws or with any applicable regulations in connection with its performance of this Agreement,' this change limits the scope of WPT's indemnification obligations. Initially, WPT was responsible for protecting Zynga from claims arising from WPT's breach of the agreement and WPT's non-compliance with laws. Now, WPT only indemnifies for breaches of the agreement itself, meaning that if Zynga faces legal issues due to WPT's failure to comply with laws during the contract's performance, Zynga wouldn't be automatically indemnified under this section. This omission is an in-text contradiction with Section 4a(iv), where each party represents they will not infringe or misappropriate third-party rights or violate laws in the Principal Territories.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a. Each party represents and warrants to the other as follows: (i) it is duly authorized under applicable law and has the authority to enter into and perform this Agreement; (ii) this Agreement constitutes a valid and binding obligation of such party enforceable in accordance with its terms; (iii) the making of this Agreement by such party does not violate any agreement, right or obligation existing between such party and any third party; (iv) the marketing and promotional activities in Exhibit A shall not infringe or misappropriate third party rights, including, without limitation, any patent, trade name, trademark, copyright or other intellectual property or proprietary right and shall not invade or violate any right of privacy, publicity, personal or proprietary right, or other common law or statutory right, nor defame any person or entity in the United States and European Union (the "Principal Territories"), and to the knowledge of such party, outside the Principal Territories; provided that such party makes no representations regarding the Licensed Property or any other materials provided by Licensor as contemplated under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text required WPT to indemnify Zynga for both breaches of the agreement and non-compliance with laws. The changed text omits the non-compliance clause, thus narrowing WPT's indemnification obligations. This creates a direct conflict with section 'a(iv)' which represents that each party will not violate laws in the Principal Territories. The omission in the changed text means WPT is no longer explicitly bound to indemnify Zynga for legal issues arising from WPT's violation of laws as it relates to the contract's performance, directly contradicting the assurance given in a(iv) and creating potential unenforceability issues. Thus this is a strong in-text contradiction.
